close_reason: Already complete: recurring list uses recurrenceDescription (commit 78e43bf), shows category icons, next dates, toggle, delete. Empty state CTA added in this session.
description: Tasks 10-11: Update recurring list display, final verification
title: Recurring list + cleanup

description: Closing modals with unsaved data does not prompt user. Track dirty state and show Alert before dismissing.
title: Discard Changes Confirmation

description: Double-redirect from index to tabs to dashboard; hardcoded header icon colors. Fix redirect and use nav theme colors.
title: Fix Navigation Inconsistencies

close_reason: Guarded Appearance.setColorScheme with Platform.OS check in _layout.tsx and settings/index.tsx. Web uses document.documentElement.style.colorScheme instead. Verified via Puppeteer - no crash on theme toggle or subsequent navigation.
description: Appearance.setColorScheme() from React Native doesn't exist on web. Once a user toggles the theme in Settings, the saved preference triggers this call on every subsequent page load via _layout.tsx lines 84-88, causing a full-screen Expo error overlay on every navigation. Guard with Platform.OS \!== 'web' check and use CSS-based approach for web theme switching.
title: Fix Appearance.setColorScheme crash on web

description: Reusable modal-list category picker with search, checkmark selection. Used by filter dropdown and add/edit forms.
title: Create CategoryPicker shared component

description: No feedback after save/delete/create operations. Create reanimated-powered toast component + context provider with showToast(message, type) hook.
title: Toast Notification System

description: Inconsistent empty states across screens. Use existing EmptyState component everywhere with icons and CTAs.
title: Standardize Empty States

description: Replace horizontal FlatList pill chips on transactions list with a dropdown button that opens CategoryPicker. Fixes funky elongation on selection.
title: Replace category filter chips with dropdown

description: Zero accessibilityLabel or accessibilityRole attributes across the entire app. WCAG compliance requires these for screen readers. This affects both web and mobile. Systematic pass adding accessibilityLabel to all Pressable/Button/Link elements, accessibilityRole to semantic elements, accessibilityHint for non-obvious gestures. Key files: All components in src/components/ui/, all screen files. This is a large effort touching many files.
title: Accessibility: Add labels and roles across all components

close_reason: Already implemented: ProgressBar.tsx uses getEnvelopeStatus() for green/yellow/red at 0-79%/80-99%/100%+ thresholds. Bars appeared flat in audit because spending was /bin/zsh.
description: Dashboard envelope cards show a plain progress bar. Add color coding: green when spending is low, yellow approaching limit, red when over budget. This gives users instant visual feedback on spending health without reading numbers.
title: Add color-coded progress bars to envelope cards

description: Add optional multi-line notes FormField to the add transaction form between category picker and recurring toggle. Wire through to onSave callback.
title: Add notes field to AddTransactionSheet

description: 15+ hardcoded hex values scattered across components. Create centralized color constants mirroring tailwind.config.js.
title: Extract Hardcoded Colors

description: On wide screens (desktop/tablet), form inputs and buttons stretch to full width making the UI look unpolished. Add max-width constraints or a centered content container so the app looks good on desktop-sized viewports while remaining full-width on mobile.
title: Web: constrain max width of inputs/buttons for desktop viewports

description: Add fullScreen prop to Modal component. AddTransactionSheet uses it so the form fills the entire screen instead of sitting below the search bar.
title: Full-screen add transaction modal

description: Dashboard uses Tabs-level header while other tabs use Stack headers, causing visual jump. Wrap Dashboard in its own Stack layout.
title: Fix Dashboard header sizing to match other tabs

close_reason: Transaction list redesigned with flat rows, dividers, single Card container
description: Flat rows with dividers instead of stacked card bubbles
title: Phase 2b: Redesign transaction list

description: Forms don't support Enter-to-submit on web. No onSubmitEditing chains between fields. Tab order works by default but could be explicit. Add onSubmitEditing to form fields that chain focus to next field, with final field triggering submit. Add returnKeyType props. Key files: src/app/sign-in.tsx, src/app/sign-up.tsx, src/components/forms/FormField.tsx, src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.tsx.
title: Web: Form keyboard UX (Enter-to-submit, field chaining)

description: AssignIncomeModal saves on close (non-standard). Add customizable action button on Modal; explicit Save for AssignIncomeModal.
title: Consistent Modal Patterns

description: Task 1: Create recurrence-rule.ts with type, describe, presets, calculator
title: RecurrenceRule type and utilities

description: Forms validate on submit only. Convert forms to react-hook-form with zod resolver and mode onBlur.
title: Real-time Form Validation

description: Epic covering all work needed to ship iBudget as an invite-only beta to family. Covers infrastructure (Supabase + PowerSync production), real auth and sync, security audit, Android + Web deployment, and gating for invite-only access.
title: Production Beta Launch (Invite-Only)

description: Create a separate Supabase project for production. Configure auth, database, and edge functions. Set up proper environment separation from dev.
title: Set up production Supabase project

description: Integrate Sentry or similar crash reporting for production. Capture unhandled exceptions, component errors, and failed network requests on web and Android. Essential for diagnosing issues in production without console access.
title: Error monitoring (Sentry integration)

description: Audit codebase for console.log statements, debug UI, and dev-only features that should not ship to production. Remove or gate behind __DEV__ flag.
title: Strip dev-only features and debug logging

description: Set up automated build pipeline. EAS Build for Android APK/AAB. Automated web deployment (currently GitHub Pages). Consider GitHub Actions for CI (lint, typecheck, test) on PRs.
title: CI/CD pipeline (EAS Build for Android + web deploy)

description: Verify Google Play Developer account access (may have existing account). Create app listing, upload screenshots, write description. Prepare for internal testing track (good for invite-only beta). Ensure app meets Play Store policies.
title: Google Play Store prep

description: Currently deployed to GitHub Pages. Evaluate if this is sufficient for production or if a proper hosting solution (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages) would be better for custom domain, HTTPS, performance, etc.
title: Production web deployment review

description: Ensure Supabase migrations can be applied cleanly to a fresh production database. Document migration workflow. Consider using Supabase CLI for migration management. No dev seed data should run in production.
title: Database migration strategy for production

description: Create a minimal privacy policy for the beta. Can use an auto-generator (e.g., termly.io, iubenda). Covers what data is collected (email, budget data), how it's stored (Supabase/PostgreSQL), no third-party data sharing. Required for Play Store. Low effort for family beta but good practice.
title: Basic privacy policy

description: Optional guided first-run experience for new users. Could be a simple series of tooltip highlights or a walkthrough modal showing key features (create household, set budget, add transactions). Nice-to-have, not required for family beta.
title: Onboarding tutorial / walkthrough

description: Research subscription/payment options for post-beta monetization. Evaluate Stripe (web), RevenueCat (mobile IAP), pricing tiers, and what features to gate. Not needed for beta launch — track for future planning.
title: Investigate paid subscriptions (post-beta)

description: Add basic analytics to understand beta engagement. Page views, feature usage, retention. Consider PostHog (open source, free tier), Mixpanel, or Supabase's built-in analytics. Helps inform product decisions post-beta.
title: Analytics for beta usage tracking

description: Create production PowerSync instance, connect to production Supabase, configure sync buckets and rules for household data isolation.
title: Set up production PowerSync

description: Supabase requires email confirmation by default. After sign-up, show a 'Check your email to confirm' screen instead of auto-redirecting to dashboard. Currently disabled for beta convenience but should be implemented properly.
title: Sign-up: add email confirmation flow

description: Sign-ups are currently disabled in Supabase dashboard for invite-only beta. When ready for public launch, re-enable sign-ups in Supabase Auth settings and set EXPO_PUBLIC_ALLOW_SIGNUP=true in production env. Also implement the email confirmation flow (see ibudget-8lc.20).
title: Re-enable public sign-up for production launch

description: Currently deployed to GitHub Pages. Investigate and set up proper hosting with a custom domain. Options: Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, or custom VPS. Need to purchase a domain name, configure DNS, set up HTTPS. Update Supabase Auth URL Configuration (Site URL + Redirect URLs) to match the new domain.
title: Set up production hosting with custom domain

description: The sync connector in src/db/connector.ts is currently a stub. Implement real PowerSync <-> Supabase sync with proper auth token handling, upload/download queues, and conflict resolution (last-write-wins for MVP).
title: Implement Supabase sync connector

description: Wire up production Supabase auth end-to-end. Currently using LocalAuthProvider for dev. Ensure SupabaseAuthProvider works correctly: sign-up, sign-in, password reset, session refresh, token management. Test the full flow on web and Android.
title: Enable real Supabase auth flow

description: Set up proper environment variable management for dev vs production. Ensure no secrets in client bundle. Configure Supabase anon key, PowerSync URL, and other env vars per environment. Consider using EAS environment configs or .env files with proper gitignore.
title: Environment configuration (dev/staging/prod)

description: The 'Load Demo Data' button in Settings should not appear in production builds. Gate behind EXPO_PUBLIC_AUTH_PROVIDER=local or a dedicated dev mode flag. Small change but important for production polish.
title: Hide demo data button in production

description: Disable public sign-up for beta. Use Supabase's built-in email invite system to add family members. May need to disable the sign-up screen or show a 'request invite' message instead. Admin invites users from Supabase dashboard.
title: Invite-only sign-up via Supabase email invites

description: Audit all Supabase tables for proper RLS policies. Ensure household data isolation (users can only access their own household's data). Verify profiles, transactions, budgets, categories, allocations all have correct policies. Critical for multi-user security.
title: Row-level security (RLS) audit

description: Add Google Sign-In as an OAuth provider through Supabase Auth. Works on both web and Android. Requires Google Cloud Console OAuth client setup and Supabase auth provider configuration. Update sign-in/sign-up screens with a 'Sign in with Google' button.
title: Google SSO (OAuth via Supabase Auth)

close_reason: Added 💰 emoji visual element above Welcome heading to reduce blank space and improve first impression.
description: The 'Welcome to iBudget / Create or join a household' screen has ~40% blank space above the heading. Content is bottom-heavy. Center it vertically or add a visual element (illustration, icon) to improve the first impression.
title: Improve welcome/setup screen layout

description: Replace default Expo icon with budgeting-themed envelope+dollar icon. Generate all required sizes programmatically.

description: Replace flex-wrap chip grid in AddTransactionSheet and [id].tsx edit mode with CategoryPicker dropdown. Eliminates duplicated chip code and matches new filter UX.

description: Loading states show blank screens or plain Loading text. Create pulsing skeleton component with composable layouts.

description: Remove min(1) from description in all zod schemas. When saving, if description is blank, store the category name instead. Affects AddTransactionSheet, [id].tsx edit, and transaction.schema.ts.
title: Make description optional, fallback to category name

description: Tasks 2-4: Schema columns, useRecurringTransactions, useProcessRecurring
title: DB schema + hook updates

description: Alert.alert() maps to window.confirm() on web — an ugly browser dialog that breaks app aesthetic. Used in 14+ places (delete transaction, discard changes, leave household, load demo data, etc.). Create a ConfirmDialog component using the existing Modal.tsx that renders styled confirmation dialogs on web while keeping Alert.alert on native. Could be a utility function: confirm({ title, message, buttons }) that checks Platform.OS. Key files: All files using Alert.alert with button arrays (settings, transactions, dashboard, household).
title: Web: Custom confirmation dialog replacing window.confirm()

close_reason: Verified via Puppeteer: Added 💰 icon mark and personality subtitles to all 3 auth screens (sign-in, sign-up, forgot-password).
description: Sign-in/sign-up/forgot-password screens are clean but generic. The iBudget wordmark uses system-default bold font with no brand identity. Consider: proper logo or icon mark, subtle background treatment (gradient, pattern), more personality in subtitle text.
title: Improve auth screen branding and personality

description: Date inputs are plain text with YYYY-MM-DD format. Create custom modal-based date picker using date-fns.
title: Date Picker Component

description: Swipeable from react-native-gesture-handler doesn't work on web. The swipe hint ('Swipe left to delete') displays misleadingly on web. Transactions have no visible delete option on web outside the detail screen. Hide swipe hint on web. Add a visible delete button (trash icon) on hover for web, or show it always on web. Alternatively, right-click context menu. Key files: src/components/transactions/TransactionItem.tsx, src/app/(tabs)/transactions/index.tsx.
title: Web: Swipe-to-delete fallback for transactions

close_reason: Already complete: PresetPicker, CustomRecurrenceModal, AddTransactionSheet integration all exist and working (commits dd7f8da, 78e43bf).
description: Tasks 7-9: PresetPicker, CustomRecurrenceModal, AddTransactionSheet integration
title: Recurrence UI components

close_reason: Verified via Puppeteer: household data persists across page reloads. Fix: hadUser ref prevents disconnectAndClear during initial auth loading.
description: After creating a household from the Dashboard, the Envelopes, Transactions, and Household tabs still show 'Set up a household first' while the Dashboard correctly shows categories. Likely a stale query or missing reactivity in useHousehold(). May be web-only or a broader reactivity issue.
title: Fix household state not syncing across tabs on web

close_reason: Fixed: baseUrl was under web.baseUrl but Expo CLI reads from experiments.baseUrl. Moved it, which properly inlines EXPO_BASE_URL in the bundle for route matching. Also removed the sed post-processing hack since experiments.baseUrl handles all asset path prefixing.
description: GitHub Pages at chaz8081.github.io/ibudget/ loads a blank white page. Console shows 3 script loading failures: all reference /_expo/static/js/web/... instead of /ibudget/_expo/static/js/web/... The baseUrl in app.json is set to /ibudget but the exported build isn't using it for asset paths. Need to: 1) verify expo export --platform web respects baseUrl, 2) set up proper GitHub Actions CI/CD for deployment, 3) ensure WASM and static assets are also correctly pathed.
title: Fix GitHub Pages blank page — asset paths missing /ibudget prefix

description: No indication that transactions can be swiped to delete. Add one-time hint banner persisted via expo-secure-store.
title: Swipe-to-Delete Hint

description: Tasks 5-6: Multiselect mode for CategoryPicker, update transactions filter
title: CategoryPicker multiselect + filter

description: On web/desktop, the bottom tab bar is a mobile pattern that wastes screen space. Replace it with a left-side collapsible navigation menu when viewport is wide enough (e.g., >768px). Keep bottom tabs for narrow/mobile viewports. This would make the web version feel more like a proper web app.
title: Web: collapsible sidebar navigation for desktop viewports

description: Modals (Add Transaction, Assign Income, Category Picker, Date Picker) render as full-screen or bottom-sheet on all viewports. On desktop, they should be centered with max-width (~480px) and proper border radius, not stretching edge-to-edge. In Modal.tsx, add web-specific max-width + centering when viewport is wide. Distinct from ibudget-5cr (page layout) — this covers modal sizing specifically. Key files: src/components/ui/Modal.tsx, src/components/ui/CategoryPicker.tsx, src/components/ui/DatePicker.tsx.
title: Web: Desktop-responsive modal sizing

title: Add actionable CTAs to empty states

description: Web users expect cursor:pointer on clickable elements and hover state color changes on buttons, cards, list items. No such feedback currently exists — buttons feel dead until clicked. Add cursor:pointer to Pressable style on web. Add hover color shifts via NativeWind hover: prefix or Pressable hovered state. Key files: src/components/ui/Button.tsx, src/components/ui/Card.tsx, src/components/budget/EnvelopeCard.tsx, src/components/transactions/TransactionItem.tsx, all tab bar items.
